userStories.title: Schema, DTO & i18n Extensions
userStories.description: Add `graphifyEnabled Boolean @default(false)` and `graphifyLastImportedAt DateTime?` to the Prisma Project model. Extend the `source` union in `AddDocumentDto` and `IndexDocumentInput` to include `'code'`. Surface the new fields in `ProjectResponseDto.from()` and `UpdateProjectDto`. Add i18n keys `graphifyDisabled` and `graphifyImportSuccess` to both `en/rag.json` and `zh/rag.json`. Create the Prisma migration. No service logic in this story — type foundation only.
acceptanceCriteria: Project.graphifyEnabled defaults to false when a project is created with no graphifyEnabled field
AddDocumentDto accepts source: 'code' without a validation error
AddDocumentDto rejects any source value not in ['ticket', 'doc', 'manual', 'code'] with a 400
IndexDocumentInput.source type includes 'code' as a valid literal
ProjectResponseDto.from(project) maps project.graphifyEnabled to dto.graphifyEnabled
ProjectResponseDto.from(project) maps project.graphifyLastImportedAt to dto.graphifyLastImportedAt as Date | null
UpdateProjectDto accepts graphifyEnabled: true without a validation error
i18n key rag.graphifyDisabled exists in both en/rag.json and zh/rag.json
suggestedCriteria: UpdateProjectDto treats graphifyEnabled as optional — omitting it does not cause a validation error
ProjectResponseDto includes graphifyLastImportedAt: null when the project has never been imported
i18n key rag.graphifyImportSuccess exists in both en/rag.json and zh/rag.json with non-empty values
AddDocumentDto rejects source: 'code2' (near-miss string) with a 400 validation error

userStories.title: RagService: deleteAllBySourceType and importGraphify Methods
userStories.description: Add two new methods to `RagService`. `deleteAllBySourceType(projectId, sourceType)` deletes all LanceDB documents where `source = sourceType` for the given project and returns the count of deleted rows (query count before delete). `importGraphify(projectId, nodes, links)` clears existing code nodes, builds embeddable content strings from nodes + their link adjacency, then calls `indexDocument()` for each node with `source: 'code'`, `sourceId: node.id`, and structured metadata. Update `rag.service.spec.ts` with unit tests for both methods.
acceptanceCriteria: RagService.deleteAllBySourceType(projectId, 'code') deletes all records where source = 'code' for the given project and returns the count of records deleted
RagService.deleteAllBySourceType(projectId, 'code') returns 0 when no source:'code' records exist
RagService.importGraphify(projectId, nodes, links) calls deleteAllBySourceType(projectId, 'code') before indexing any nodes
RagService.importGraphify(projectId, nodes, []) with an empty links array indexes each node with content "{type} {label} in {source_file}"
RagService.importGraphify(projectId, nodes, links) builds content that includes "{relation} {neighbor_label}" for each link where the node is the source
RagService.importGraphify(projectId, nodes, links) calls indexDocument() with source: 'code', sourceId: node.id, and metadata containing label, type, source_file, community
RagService.importGraphify(projectId, [], []) returns { imported: 0, cleared: N } where N is the count from deleteAllBySourceType
suggestedCriteria: A node with no source_file produces content "{type} {label}" without an 'in ...' segment
A node with no type field uses 'node' as the default type in the content string
A node that is only a link target (not a source) does not have link text appended to its content
importGraphify returns { imported: nodes.length, cleared } where imported equals the total node count passed in
deleteAllBySourceType does not delete records with a different source value (e.g. 'ticket') for the same project

userStories.title: Import Endpoint: POST /projects/:slug/kb/import/graphify
userStories.description: Create `ImportGraphifyDto`, `GraphifyNodeDto`, and `GraphifyLinkDto` in a new `apps/api/src/rag/dto/import-graphify.dto.ts`. Add `POST kb/import/graphify` route to `RagController` guarded to ADMIN role. Route calls `ragService.importGraphify()` and on success writes `graphifyLastImportedAt` to the project record via Prisma. Add e2e coverage for happy-path, 403 non-admin, and 400 graphify-disabled cases.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /projects/:slug/kb/import/graphify with a valid body and ADMIN auth returns { ret: 0, data: { imported: number, cleared: number } }
POST /projects/:slug/kb/import/graphify returns 403 when the caller does not have ADMIN role
POST /projects/:slug/kb/import/graphify returns 404 when the project slug does not exist
POST /projects/:slug/kb/import/graphify returns 400 when project.graphifyEnabled is false
POST /projects/:slug/kb/import/graphify with nodes: [] returns { imported: 0, cleared: 0 } and does not call indexDocument
ImportGraphifyDto rejects a body missing nodes with a 400 validation error
ImportGraphifyDto accepts a body with nodes present and links absent (links optional)
The import route calls ragService.importGraphify(project.id, dto.nodes, dto.links ?? []) and returns its result
After a successful import, the controller updates project.graphifyLastImportedAt to the current UTC timestamp via Prisma
POST /projects/:slug/kb/import/graphify response includes graphifyLastImportedAt in the project's next GET /projects/:slug response
suggestedCriteria: ImportGraphifyDto rejects a body where nodes is a string (not an array) with a 400 validation error
The route rejects a request with an invalid JWT with a 401
When ragService.importGraphify throws an unexpected error, the controller propagates the exception without swallowing it
graphifyLastImportedAt is written as a UTC timestamp (not a local time)

userStories.title: ProjectsService: Purge Code Nodes on graphifyEnabled Toggle-Off
userStories.description: Inject `RagService` into `ProjectsService`. In `ProjectsService.update()`, detect when `graphifyEnabled` transitions from `true` to `false` in the update payload and call `ragService.deleteAllBySourceType(projectId, 'code')`. If the delete call throws, log at warn level and continue — the flag change persists regardless. Import `RagModule` into `ProjectsModule` to satisfy DI. Add unit tests for all toggle-transition scenarios.
acceptanceCriteria: ProjectsService.update() calls ragService.deleteAllBySourceType(projectId, 'code') when graphifyEnabled changes from true to false in the update payload
ProjectsService.update() does not call ragService.deleteAllBySourceType when graphifyEnabled is not present in the update payload
ProjectsService.update() does not call ragService.deleteAllBySourceType when graphifyEnabled changes from false to true
ProjectsService.update() does not call ragService.deleteAllBySourceType when graphifyEnabled is true in both current state and update payload
When ragService.deleteAllBySourceType throws, ProjectsService.update() logs a warning at warn level and does not re-throw (the flag change is persisted regardless)
RagModule is imported in ProjectsModule so RagService is available via DI in ProjectsService
suggestedCriteria: ProjectsService.update() does not call ragService.deleteAllBySourceType when graphifyEnabled changes from false to false (redundant update)
The warn log includes the projectId and the error message when deleteAllBySourceType throws
Importing RagModule into ProjectsModule does not introduce a circular dependency

userStories.title: CLI: koda kb import --graphify Command
userStories.description: Add an `import` sub-command to `apps/cli/src/commands/kb.ts`. The command accepts `--project <slug>` and `--graphify <path>` (both required) plus an optional `--json` flag. It resolves auth, reads and parses the graph.json file, extracts `nodes` and `links`, calls the generated import API client method, and prints results. Missing flags exit 3; file-not-found or invalid JSON exits 1; missing auth exits 2; success exits 0. Add unit tests to `kb.spec.ts`. Run `bun run generate` before implementing to get the generated method name.
acceptanceCriteria: koda kb import --project koda --graphify ./graph.json exits 0 and prints '✓ Graphify import complete: {imported} code nodes indexed ({cleared} cleared)' to stdout
koda kb import --project koda --graphify ./graph.json --json exits 0 and prints raw JSON to stdout
koda kb import --project koda --graphify ./missing.json exits 1 and writes a file-not-found message to stderr
koda kb import --project koda --graphify ./invalid.json (non-JSON content) exits 1 and writes a parse-error message to stderr
koda kb import with missing --project exits 3
koda kb import with missing --graphify exits 3
When auth config is absent, exits 2 without calling the API
The command sends { nodes, links } extracted from the parsed file — it does not send other top-level keys from the graph.json
suggestedCriteria: When links is absent from the parsed graph.json, the command sends { nodes, links: [] } or { nodes } to the API (no undefined key)
When the API returns a non-zero ret code, the command calls handleApiError and exits non-zero
koda kb import --graphify ./graph.json with no --project exits 3 (project flag is required, not inferred from context)

analysis:
This feature extends Koda's knowledge base with code-structure documents from Graphify's knowledge graph. The implementation is a pure extension — no existing retrieval paths change. Key findings from codebase analysis:

**Schema**: Project model has `autoIndexOnClose` and `autoAssign` boolean fields (both `@default(false)`) — `graphifyEnabled` follows the same pattern. SQLite in dev, PostgreSQL/MySQL supported; no native enums, use string fields. Soft deletes via `deletedAt`.

**Source union**: `add-document.dto.ts` currently validates `source` as `@IsIn(['ticket', 'doc', 'manual'])`. The `IndexDocumentInput` interface in `rag.service.ts` has a matching `source` literal union. Both must be extended to include `'code'`.

**DTOs**: All use class-validator with `@IsOptional()` + i18n error message strings. `ProjectResponseDto` has a static `from()` that maps Prisma objects to response shape — must add `graphifyEnabled` and `graphifyLastImportedAt`.

**RagService**: Injectable class with `indexDocument()` and `deleteBySource()` (deletes by `sourceId`). The new `deleteAllBySourceType()` deletes by the `source` column (not `sourceId`), returning the count. LanceDB table access is internal to `RagService` — new methods follow the same internal patterns.

**RagController**: Uses a private `resolveProject(slug)` helper that returns the project or throws `NotFoundAppException`. Admin-guarded routes call `@CurrentUser()` and check `user.role === 'ADMIN'`. The import route must follow this same pattern. After a successful import, `graphifyLastImportedAt` is written via Prisma directly in the controller.

**ProjectsService**: Has `update()` which validates, checks uniqueness, updates via Prisma. Needs `RagService` injected to call `deleteAllBySourceType` when `graphifyEnabled` flips false. Failure must log at warn level and NOT re-throw. `ProjectsModule` must import `RagModule` (verify `RagModule` exports `RagService`).

**CLI**: Commander.js sub-commands on the `kb` parent. Uses `resolveContext({ projectSlug })` (not `resolveAuth`) for auth resolution. Calls generated client methods via `unwrap()`. Human output uses `✓` prefix; JSON output via `JSON.stringify`. Exit codes: 0=success, 1=file error, 2=auth missing, 3=missing flag. The generated import method name must be looked up in `apps/cli/src/generated/index.ts` after `bun run generate` — implementer should check the generated file.

**Dependency order**: US-001 (schema/DTOs/i18n) → US-002 (RagService methods) → US-003 (endpoint) + US-004 (toggle cleanup) → US-005 (CLI). US-003 and US-004 can be parallelized. US-005 requires the endpoint from US-003 AND a `bun run generate` to produce the generated client method.

**Migration**: Standard Prisma migration via `bun run db:migrate`. The two new columns (`graphifyEnabled Boolean @default(false)` and `graphifyLastImportedAt DateTime?`) are backward-compatible — existing rows get `false` and `null` respectively.

**Risk areas**: (1) LanceDB delete-by-source implementation — verify the internal table schema has a `source` column and that a delete-by-column query is supported. (2) The generated CLI client method name is unknown until `bun run generate` runs after US-003 — implementer must discover it from the generated file. (3) Circular DI risk: `ProjectsModule` importing `RagModule` — verify `RagModule` does not import `ProjectsModule`.
